TextENGNew: ``Hellooo!!@k@vS06/06/150600464. Working hard in this heat, I see!``

``Oh, if it isn't Mion-san.@k@vS06/10/151000002. Good afternoon.@k@vS06/10/151000003. Oh, are those refreshments?@k@vS06/10/151000004. How generous!@k@vS06/10/151000005. That's our team manager for you!``

``Well, I just felt like it.@k@vS06/06/150600466. I didn't buy these for you specifically, Coach, so don't go getting the wrong idea.@k@vS06/06/150600467. And stop casually drafting me as your manager without my permission.``
``If you were our manager, Mion, I think we'd all feel a lot more confident.``
``...Come on, do it, Mion.@k@vS06/07/151200118. Be our manager.``
Satoshi-kun said it with a genuinely carefree smile, beaming at me.
...Being an official manager sounded like way too much hassle. I absolutely didn't want that.
But I'd been coming every single week without fail to cheer them on, so calling myself a quasi-manager at this point wouldn't really be wrong.
``Weell, if I ever actually feel like it, maybe.@k@vS06/06/150600469. So, how's it going?@k@vS06/06/150600470. What's Satoshi-kun's batting average looking like?``

``Oh yes, yes, he's been working hard.@k@vS06/10/151000007. His batting average? Hmm, let me think...@k@vS06/10/151000008. If you go to Sevens Mart and buy any three bags of rice crackers, you see...``

``Ah ha ha ha ha!@k@vS06/06/150600472. So in other words, that means thirty percent?@k@vS06/06/150600473. ...Huh, a .300 hitter!@k@vS06/06/150600474. Satoshi-kun, you're a pretty impressive batter, aren't you!``
changeReason: New translation adds '.300 hitter' phrasing and 'impressive batter' exclamation matching JPN 三割打者 and 高打率打者 that the original reduces to just 'you're doing great.'

TextENGNew: ``...Mmph.@k@vS06/07/151200120. Batting average isn't just about the numbers, Mion.@k@vS06/07/151200121. If you can't deliver when it counts, the stats are meaningless.``
``Satoshi-kun seems to understand his own weaknesses well.@k@vS06/10/151000010. If you're that self-aware, then all that's left is to practice.``
When the coach gave the signal, the pitcher wound up big and released the white ball.
Satoshi-kun tracked the trajectory calmly with his eyes, and when the ball entered his zone, he swung with everything he had.
CRACK!!
With a satisfying crack, the ball shot through the gap between first and second base and rolled far, far away.
``Ohhh!@k@vS06/06/150600476. Nice one!@k@vS06/06/150600477. Clap clap clap clap!``
changeReason: Original fabricates 'Good job, Satoshi-kun!' not in JPN; JPN has onomatopoeia ぱちぱちぱち (clapping sounds) which new correctly renders.

TextENGNew: I applauded without reserve, and Satoshi-kun, as if to hide his embarrassment, pulled his helmet down lower over his eyes.
``Hmm.@k@vS06/10/151000012. In free batting and when the game's going smooth, he shows really solid, composed hitting, but...``
The coach smiled with a hint of regret.
changeReason: Original says 'compassionately' but JPN 残念そうに means 'regretfully/with disappointment'; new translation corrects the emotion.

TextENGNew: ...Yes, Satoshi-kun's weakness was that he couldn't perform when the pressure was on.
Normally he showed genuinely good play, but he crumbled at the critical moments when everyone's hopes rode on him.
I didn't think it was stage fright exactly... but somehow, he just couldn't produce his usual skill.
...It fit the character of Satoshi-kun, who seemed composed on the surface but was actually rather spacey underneath.

``On the scoreboard and the stat sheets, you're practically an elite hitter.@k@vS06/06/150600479. ...But choking under pressure might be a fatal flaw.@k@vS06/06/150600480. ...Hmm, Coach, what do you think?``
changeReason: New translation omits the Ukita Jaguars match reference and the closing scolding line present in the JPN.

TextENGNew: ``...Mmph...@k@vS06/07/151200123. Knock it off. I know, so just drop it already...``
``If he'd been able to connect there... it would've been quite the heroic moment...@k@vS06/10/151000014. What a shame.``
One hit could've turned the game around, or at worst forced extra innings!@kThat was the thrilling moment when Satoshi-kun's turn at bat had come.
I wanted so badly for him to rise to everyone's expectations and bloom on the big stage...!
changeReason: Subject changed: original attributes the wish to 'Irie' (third person) but JPN uses first person (ほしい); new correctly uses 'I wanted.'

TextENGNew: But given his track record... the odds of Satoshi-kun delivering in that spot were painfully low...
The coach had overruled the bench's calls for a pinch hitter and stubbornly stuck with Satoshi-kun's at-bat.
``Aaagh!@k@vS06/06/150600482. It would've been perfect if you'd come through right there!!@k@vS06/06/150600483. Why can't you do it?!``
changeReason: New translation truncates the second rhetorical question about why he can't hit under pressure, present in JPN.

TextENGNew: ``It can't be helped...@k@vS06/07/151200125. I really am weak in those situations...@k@vS06/07/151200126. Honestly, I would've understood if they'd sent in a pinch hitter...``
``It's not about whether the team wins -- it's about whether you win, Satoshi-kun!!@k@vS06/06/150600486. Aaagh!!@k@vS06/06/150600487. I'm so frustrated!``
changeReason: New translation omits the JPN lines about lacking fighting spirit (闘争心) and the rhetorical question.

TextENGNew: ``When they're about to sub someone in for you, that's when you should insist 'Let me bat!'@k@vS06/06/150600490. Asking to be taken out yourself? What kind of attitude is that?!``
changeReason: New translation omits the lament about lacking fighting spirit (なんでここまで闘争心がないのかなぁ) from JPN.

TextENGNew: ``That's what I'm always telling him too...@k@vS06/10/151000016. Well, that's part of what makes Satoshi-kun who he is, I suppose...``
``Be a gentleman off the field, but be a demon in the game!!@k@vS06/06/150600491_2. I think a man can have that kind of duality!!@k@vS06/06/150600491_3. Two faces, that's totally fine!``
changeReason: New translation omits the concluding line 'Satoshi-kun has no demon!' (悟史くんには鬼がない) present in JPN.

TextENGNew: ``You're from Hinamizawa, aren't you? Channel your ancestors' demon blood once in a while!@k@vS06/06/150600491_5. ...Hey, are you even listening?!@k@vS06/06/150600491_6. Satoshi-kun!!``
``I don't really get it, but, um...``
``What don't you get?! We're talking about you here!``
Satoshi-kun was patting my head, looking absolutely delighted as he did.
changeReason: Original adds 'suddenly' and 'for some reason' not in JPN; new translation more faithfully renders the original Japanese.

TextENGNew: ...And so abruptly, too.@kAs always, I went bright red and lost the power of speech...
``You're saying all that for my sake, right, Mion?@k@vS06/07/151200137. ...Thanks.@k@vS06/07/151200138. I'll do my best.``
``...S-Sa... Satoshi-kun.@k@vS06/06/150600496. Um, don't try to... distract me like that...``
``I'm not trying to distract you.@k@vS06/07/151200140. I'm just happy that you care.@k@vS06/07/151200141. Ahahahaha.``
Face burning crimson, all I could do was surrender my head to him.
changeReason: Original reduces JPN's 真っ赤になりながら (turning bright red) to just 'couldn't resist'; new preserves the blushing detail.

TextENGNew: ...Letting out one of his trademark 'mmph's as I did.
After Satoshi-kun called out 'I'll do my best!' and ran back to the field, all that remained was me, face scarlet, steam practically rising from my head.
``...You really can't handle having your head stroked, can you?``
changeReason: Different meaning: original asks 'feel good?' but JPN 弱い means 'weak/vulnerable to'; new correctly conveys 'can't handle it.'

TextENGNew: ``...Depends on who's doing the stroking.@k@vS06/06/150600498. If you tried it, Coach, I'd put you in a joint lock, so be warned.``
``Aww, no fair!@k@vS06/10/151000019. Ah ha ha ha...@k@vS06/10/151000020. Alright, everyone gather up!!@k@vS06/10/151000021. Let's split into teams!``
The Hinamizawa Fighters were, as baseball teams go, firmly in the 'casual' category.
Rather than serious training aimed at tournament victories, the emphasis was on scraping together enough players and having fun playing ball.
Compared to the Okinomiya Titans, who practiced on the same field on different days with dumbbells, barbells, and endless bunny-hops in full sports-drama fashion, the contrast was striking.
Teams were split by rock-paper-scissors and grade level, and the game began in a thoroughly laid-back atmosphere.
``Well, guess I'll keep score.@k@vS06/06/150600500. Coach, got a pencil anywhere?``
``Thanks for always helping out.@k@vS06/10/151000023. Here, please take care of it.``
I accepted the writing utensils and scorebook with practiced ease.
...At first, I couldn't even tell the difference between recording a hit and an error, but by now I'd gotten pretty good at it.
``Go for it, Satoshi-kuuun!!@k@vS06/06/150600502. Oh wait, if I cheer too hard, it'll just make you choke again, won't it?@k@vS06/06/150600503. ...How am I supposed to root for you?``
``...Mmph... that's not very encouraging either...!``
``Ahahahaha! Do your best!!``
The gentle hours of a Sunday afternoon drifted by.
The sky was clear, and the grandeur of the towering white clouds reminded me how wholesomely we were spending our time.
Humans really are made to be active under the sun.
One of the baseball-loving parents had volunteered to umpire today,@kso the coach was sitting quietly on the bench.
He called out encouragement to the children as they scattered to their defensive positions.
Most coaches would be barking orders and scolding players at a time like this, but our coach was more like a kindergarten teacher.
He let everyone play freely, watching them with a gentle smile.
...Other baseball teams would probably call it slack...
``Even so, it's impressive how well everyone moves with such a modest amount of practice.``

``Kids in Hinamizawa run around and play hard every day.@k@vS06/10/151000025. Their daily exercise is on a completely different level from town kids.@k@vS06/10/151000026. So my training philosophy is just to let them enjoy themselves and draw out their natural energy.``
``...Wow, that's the polar opposite of the Okinomiya Titans' philosophy.@k@vS06/06/150600507. No wonder you and their coach butt heads.``
``The Titans' approach isn't wrong.@k@vS06/10/151000028. Matches exist to be won, after all.@k@vS06/10/151000029. I just don't consider winning the ultimate goal, is all.``
``I think it's wonderful when everyone bands together and strives toward a common goal.@k@vS06/10/151000031. ...It just so happens that for us, that goal isn't always about winning, you see...``
The Hinamizawa Fighters and the Okinomiya Titans had been rivals for quite some time.
Part of it was sharing the same school field for practice, but...@kno, originally they'd been the same team, and the Hinamizawa members had split off to form their own. That's where it had started.
Well, anyway, the point is:@kthey were rivals.
The win-at-all-costs Okinomiya Titans versus the 'as long as we're having fun' Hinamizawa Fighters.
...But surprisingly, their win-loss record against each other was perfectly even. They were genuinely matched in strength.
changeReason: New translation adds the detail about being 'genuinely matched in strength' (実力は伯仲) that the original omits.

TextENGNew: Which meant our team possessed something that put us on par with a victory-obsessed squad.
What that something was... even if I couldn't put it into words, I had a vague sense of it.
``Come ooon, Red Team, step it up!@k@vS06/06/150600509. A two-point deficit is totally catchable!@k@vS06/06/150600510. Don't you dare give up yet!``
It was true that some parents criticized the coach's lack of intensity in his guidance.
...But there were also a few who resonated with his approach.@kWhich side was I on?@kI'd like to say the latter... but I'd hold off on that judgment for now.
Because this man's occasional bizarre tendencies raised some serious questions about his fitness as an educator. (wry laugh)
changeReason: Original says 'sexual harassment' but JPN ヘンな一面 means 'strange/weird side'; new translation is more accurate to the source.

TextENGNew: The fact that he was an unparalleled maid enthusiast... well, that was a story for another time.
Satoshi-kun carefully studied the pitch, letting two balls pass, then connected with a precise swing and made it safely to first base.
``Oh, Satoshi-kun got a hit!@k@vS06/06/150600512. When he's relaxed, he's really impressive.``
``At first, he'd get worn out after just a few practice swings.@k@vS06/10/151000033. Hahaha, thinking back on that, he's really come a long way.``
``...Satoshi-kun really does seem like the bookish type.@k@vS06/06/150600514. It's kind of surprising that someone like him decided to play baseball.``
Normally, any kid who joins a youth baseball team is, without exception, someone who loves baseball.
They play pickup games on their own time. So they come in with a baseline of athletic sense and stamina.
But from what Sis told me, Satoshi-kun didn't usually move around much at school.@kGiven free time, he was the type to read a book.
The connection between someone like him and the Hinamizawa Fighters baseball team was hard to figure out.
``Ahahahahaha.@k@vS06/10/151000035. That's because I'm the one who invited Satoshi-kun to join this team.``
``Oh?@k@vS06/06/150600516. You personally scouted him?@k@vS06/06/150600517. ...Did you see some amazing raw talent in him, or what?``
``Oh, excuse me, could I get a cup of barley tea?@k@vS06/10/151000037. ...Ah, thanks.``
The coach asked for tea at a suspiciously convenient moment.
...This coach might joke around constantly, but I knew he was actually a composed, mature adult.
So I could tell he was trying to dodge the subject...
``...Is there some particular reason behind it?``
The coach tried to deflect with comments about how good the tea was, but when he realized I wasn't going to let it go, he gave a small shrug and answered.
``...I think you, Sonozaki-san, might be able to guess.``
...A reason that Sonozaki Mion would be able to guess.
...I raced through the database on Satoshi-kun that had been built from Mion's side of things...
changeReason: New translation includes 'from Mion's side of things' matching JPN 魅音サイドから, a detail the original omits.

TextENGNew: The only thing that matched the coach's darkly suggestive tone was... something to do with Satoshi-kun's family.
Satoshi-kun had lost both parents in an accident the year before last.
Since then, life at his uncle's house had been difficult -- that was common knowledge.
``...So, Coach, you invited him onto the team... to give him an outlet? To help him blow off steam?``
``Sports are wonderful.@k@vS06/10/151000040. Moving your body and working up a good sweat is an excellent way to release stress.@k@vS06/10/151000041. ...That's exactly what Satoshi-kun needed.``
``...Um, well, you know how Satoshi-kun is... kind of spacey, or carefree, or however you'd put it?@k@vS06/06/150600521. ...I'd honestly never have guessed he was under that much pressure...``
I had a vague sense that Satoshi-kun was enduring considerable hardship behind that smile of his.
But Sonozaki Mion didn't seem to be aware of such things, so I gave that kind of answer instead.

``After he was taken in by his uncle, Satoshi-kun's health deteriorated considerably.@k@vS06/10/151000043. He'd complain of all sorts of symptoms, but no matter how thoroughly I examined him, I couldn't find any physical cause.``
Shortly after being taken into the uncle's household,@kSatoshi-kun had begun to fall ill frequently.
Chronic fatigue and headaches.
Listlessness and disrupted sleep patterns.
...The coach -- no, Dr. Irie -- eventually realized:@kthis wasn't a disease of the body. It was a disease of the mind.
That life in the uncle's house was far from harmonious had quickly become gossip throughout Hinamizawa.
Before the shock of losing his parents had even faded, being thrust into that environment... it was only natural that his mind would buckle under the strain.
``Satoshi-kun is the type who can't let his emotions burst out.@k@vS06/10/151000045. Whether it's crying or getting angry -- he's especially bad at that kind of emotional release.``
``...That's true. I really can't picture Satoshi-kun crying or getting angry...``
``...Did you know that Satoshi-kun's mother had been married multiple times?``
Satoshi-kun's mother had gone through several remarriages.
In other words, he'd experienced a succession of unfamiliar fathers.
Having welcomed multiple unknown adults into the sacred space of his family home, Satoshi-kun had inevitably been forced to grow up before his time.

"Satoshi-kun is emotionally precocious.@k@vS06/10/151000048. He probably isn't aware of it himself, though.@k@vS06/10/151000049. ...Crying, laughing, throwing tantrums.@k@vS06/10/151000050. He was forced to graduate from that stage far too quickly, and became an adult."
changeReason: Critical mistranslation corrected: original says 'mentally underdeveloped' but JPN 精神的に早熟 means 'emotionally precocious' -- opposite meaning.

TextENGNew: ``...The way you put it, Coach, being 'grown-up' doesn't exactly sound like a good thing.``
``Children are designed to grow by living like children.@k@vS06/10/151000052. If you force them to be adults too early... nothing good comes of it.``
The friction between Satoko and the aunt was well-known.@k...But from what I'd heard, she hadn't gotten along with any of the remarried fathers either.
Satoko had been causing discord in the family for as long as anyone could remember...
And Satoshi-kun, trying to shield her as her brother, had perhaps at some point taken on the role of her protector.
``...That's why I thought the physical symptoms Satoshi-kun developed were an SOS -- his body crying out that it couldn't endure any more.``
And so the coach had invited Satoshi-kun onto his baseball team.
It's a brand-new team, we're short on members.@kPlease, join us!@k...Something along those lines.
``He was reluctant at first.@k@vS06/10/151000055. ...But as we talked during his appointments, he gradually opened up.``
``...I suggested he just come watch a practice.@k@vS06/10/151000057. Try picking up a bat just once.@k@vS06/10/151000058. Try just throwing a ball around.``
Sports had been genuinely beneficial for Satoshi-kun, who'd been on the verge of mental breakdown.
His condition improved visibly, and he began to take a genuine interest in the game of baseball.
His swing took on proper form, gradually aligning with textbook technique...@kHe started reading pitches better, too.
``...Ah, that makes sense.@k@vS06/06/150600527. Satoshi-kun is the methodical type.@k@vS06/06/150600528. He probably studied the how-to manuals and taught himself through theory.``
``And before you knew it, he was a .300 hitter.@k@vS06/10/151000058_2. He really has worked incredibly hard.@k@vS06/10/151000059. I'm genuinely proud of him.``
``Are you and Satoshi-kun close, Coach?``
``I don't think we're on bad terms.@k@vS06/10/151000060. ...The fact that he confides his worries and troubles in me... I take that to mean he trusts me.``
When the coach declared so confidently that he'd earned Satoshi-kun's trust, I felt a small pang of jealousy.
Satoshi-kun and I were friends, but we weren't intimate enough to confide our troubles in each other.
``...Oh my.@k@vS06/10/151000062. Are you jealous?``
``Wha?!@k@vS06/06/150600535. Wh-Wh-What... jealous of what?!``
Caught completely off guard by his direct hit, I went scarlet and floundered.
The coach could plainly see he'd struck a nerve and burst out laughing.
``...Please do be there for Satoshi-kun as someone he can confide in, Sonozaki-san.``
changeReason: Original generalizes to 'stay as his good friend' but JPN 相談相手 means 'someone to confide in'; new is more specific and accurate.

TextENGNew: ``W-Well... ahahahaha, if I could be that for him, that'd be... nice, I suppose...!@k@vS06/06/150600537. Ahaha...``
All I could manage was to laugh off my own incoherent words.
``...About Satoshi-kun, though.@k@vS06/10/151000065. ...Lately, he seems to be under the weather again.``
CRACK!
A sharp crack rang out, and the white ball soared into the sky above left field.
...Despite the satisfying sound, Satoshi-kun's well-struck ball was easily caught for an out.
Apparently, the relationship between Satoko and the aunt had deteriorated severely as of late.
...The connection was obvious.
``It seems the aunt has been bullying Satoko-chan in some really petty, un-adult-like ways.@k@vS06/10/151000067. ...Satoko-chan is... she's really in a terrible state, the poor thing.``
``And.@k@vS06/06/150600540. Satoshi-kun, who keeps shielding her... he's falling apart too, is that it?``
I had no interest in how badly Satoko was doing, so I steered the conversation right back to Satoshi-kun.
...I didn't really know much about this 'Satoko' person.
I was aware of her as one element of Satoshi-kun's environment, but... I'd never taken a deep interest in what kind of person she actually was.
A sister who found her sense of purpose by fussing over her spacey brother.@kThat was about the extent of what I knew.
...But given Satoshi-kun's personality... I was starting to suspect that even his spaciness might be an act for his sister's sake.
By deliberately playing the role of the hapless brother... wasn't he giving his sister a purpose and a role to fill?@kAnd not consciously -- unconsciously.
Satoshi-kun was... surprisingly capable when he was on his own.
I'd only noticed that recently.@k...And that's what made me feel so strongly this way.
...In other words, the person called 'Satoshi-kun' was constantly being shaped and constrained by the person called 'Satoko.'
Satoshi-kun himself, while experiencing some stress, had apparently managed to get along reasonably well with the various men his mother remarried.
Even now, he wasn't stirring up nearly as much friction with the aunt as Satoko was.
...It was because Satoshi-kun always had to shield Satoko... that he was forced to endure such hardship.
I didn't know what kind of kid Satoko was.@k...But I couldn't deny that a quiet hostility toward her had begun to take root inside me.
...I admitted to myself, at least partly, that it was jealousy -- jealousy that she monopolized Satoshi-kun's existence.
``Lately, the aunt's bullying of Satoko-chan seems to have escalated quite a bit.@k@vS06/10/151000070. ...And Satoshi-kun's declining health appears to be in direct proportion.``
The coach let out a sigh.
Now that he mentioned it... Satoshi-kun had been noticeably absent from practice more often lately.
...Looking back, he'd seemed a bit low on energy in general.
When he was with everyone, he acted normal, but... when he was alone, he'd hang his head... and seemed to do nothing but sigh...
``...Coach, you invited Satoshi-kun to play baseball to save him from being crushed by stress, didn't you?``
``Yes.@k@vS06/10/151000072. That was the idea.``
``...And now, even while playing baseball.@k@vS06/06/150600543. Satoshi-kun is being crushed by stress again... So what do you invite him to do this time?``
``...............Hahaha.@k@vS06/10/151000074. What should we do, I wonder...``
The coach smiled, but there was no strength in it.
...Seeing this weak, helpless gesture from the man Satoshi-kun supposedly trusted, the man who was supposed to be someone he could lean on... I couldn't hide my small disappointment.
``Keep this between us, okay?@k@vS06/10/151000076. ...The truth is, Satoshi-kun...@k@vS06/10/151000077. ...he's told me he wants to quit the team.``
``He said he's tired, supposedly.@k@vS06/10/151000079. ...But that's just the surface reason.@k@vS06/10/151000080. ...He wants to spend more time with Satoko-chan.``

``He worries that while he's at baseball practice, Satoko-chan might be getting bullied by the aunt.@k@vS06/10/151000082. The guilt of not being by her side during those moments... that's what's eating at him.``
changeReason: New translation expands on the guilt of not being by Satoko's side, more faithfully rendering the JPN's emotional detail.

TextENGNew: I was speechless for a while.
...Again... Satoko.@kSatoko.
How much sacrifice would the person named 'Satoshi-kun' be forced to make for the person named 'Satoko'?
Satoshi-kun's stress could be eliminated by something astonishingly simple: just ignoring his sister.
...But I knew he could never do that.
If that was the case... then shouldn't the blame fall on his helpless little sister?
I was aware she was being terribly bullied.
But that was the consequence of her own inability to manage the relationship -- her own fault.
It was the price Satoko paid for refusing to communicate with the aunt.
If Satoko... if she were more mature, the way Satoshi-kun was, she could've gotten along reasonably well with the aunt. Things would never have deteriorated this far.
...And then none of it would have spilled over onto Satoshi-kun in the first place.
The practice match had turned lopsided without my noticing, and there was barely anything worth watching anymore.
The children on the losing team had given up, and the game had devolved into a listless affair.
The sky, too, had turned gray at some point, casting a gloomy pall over what had been a refreshing blue.
...There was no smell of rain, but the oppressive gray overhead was thoroughly dismal, and the lack of any cooling breeze only made the thin film of sweat even more unpleasant.
At some point, the coach had also fallen silent, his gaze fixed on the ground at his feet.
Meanwhile, I simply watched Satoshi-kun.
...I could no longer see him as vibrant or full of energy.
...He'd once found joy in baseball again... had recovered his vitality.
...And now, because Satoko and the aunt's relationship had worsened... he was sliding back.
...The words slipped from my lips, almost involuntarily.
``...Rather than trying to do something about Satoshi-kun, shouldn't we do something about Satoko first?``
changeReason: New translation adds 'rather than trying to do something about Satoshi-kun' matching JPN 悟史くんをどうこうするってより, a comparison the original omits.

TextENGNew: It was barely a murmur, but it carried a weight that wouldn't let the coach simply let it pass.
``...That... what do you mean by that, Sonozaki-san?``
``It's not complicated.@k@vS06/06/150600549. ...If Satoko shaped up, she wouldn't be a burden on Satoshi-kun anymore.@k@vS06/06/150600550. Isn't that all there is to it?``
``...Hmm... that's not exactly...``
``Right?@k@vS06/06/150600552. Because Satoko keeps stirring up trouble, Satoshi-kun has to intervene and take the heat, doesn't he?@k@vS06/06/150600553. That's the root cause of all his exhaustion.``
Satoshi-kun... never flinched from any hardship.
...No matter how frightening the opponent... to protect someone... he'd summon every ounce of courage and stand his ground.@kI knew that firsthand. I'd witnessed it with my own eyes.
Whenever the aunt screamed at Satoko for some reason...
...Satoshi-kun would step between them.@kTo shield his sister.
...Which, without question, would infuriate the aunt.
...A fight Satoshi-kun had no need to buy, yet somehow it would always morph into a battle between him and the aunt.
And Satoko could just cower behind his back, probably sticking her tongue out when no one was looking.
...Never sparing a thought for how deeply he was being cut to pieces on her behalf.
Because Satoko depended too heavily on Satoshi-kun... that was precisely why he was being driven into a corner.
``Sonozaki-san... I'm surprised to hear you talk that way.@k@vS06/10/151000086. ...I thought you were Satoko-chan's friend?@k@vS06/10/151000087. Aren't you?``
changeReason: New translation truncates Irie's speech, omitting the 'big sister figure' (お姉さん的な立場) reference present in JPN.

TextENGNew: ``...I assumed you were encouraging and comforting Satoko-chan at school every day.@k@vS06/10/151000089. ...And yet for you to say something like that...``
changeReason: New translation more faithfully renders JPN's detail about comforting/encouraging Satoko at school daily.

TextENGNew: The coach was looking at me with a deeply skeptical expression I'd never seen before.
...I could easily guess what feelings he harbored toward me now.
...I felt a twinge of regret. I'd... talked too much.
Sonozaki Mion was Houjou Satoko's friend.
They were very close, and... ever since the aunt's bullying had escalated and Satoko had started struggling, Mion had been there to encourage and comfort her... or so it was supposed to be.
I had completely forgotten that and, getting carried away as 'myself,' had said far too much.
...It was only natural for the coach to look at me with suspicion.
...My comments just now could change the coach's entire perception of Sonozaki Mion.
...I would have to confess honestly to Sis later and ask her to smooth things over.
...I had truly... blundered.
And yet... I didn't think I'd said anything wrong.
I had objectively, concisely analyzed the situation Satoshi-kun found himself in, and offered a reasonable solution.
No more pretending to be a romantic girl -- I'd be honest with myself:@k...I was afraid that Satoshi-kun would leave this team, the one I'd finally become a part of.
changeReason: New translation includes the 乙女ごっこ (playing maiden) self-admission and the detail about 'finally becoming part of the team' that the original omits.

TextENGNew: I couldn't go to the school. I couldn't go to Hinamizawa.@kThis was my only point of contact with Satoshi-kun.
I could say without exaggeration that connecting with Satoshi-kun was my reason for living.
...That's right. I was in love with Satoshi-kun.
...I had no intention of confessing... no plans to pursue it.@kJust being able to spend time together was enough.
Because, given my circumstances, that was my limit.
My peculiar situation meant I couldn't reveal who I really was.
I could only exist in Okinomiya as Mion's shadow.
If it was that stifling, why not just leave Okinomiya?
...No, no, no, stupid stupid stupid!
No matter how confined my life might be.@kIf Satoshi-kun would pat my head every now and then.@k...That was infinitely better.
...How did I end up falling this hard for Satoshi-kun...?
I'd read it in a shoujo manga.
They always say love needs no reason.
...Even I was baffled sometimes, wondering why I was so hung up on him.
I exhaled long and slow, then held my breath tight.@k...Forcibly slowing my pounding heart.
As the excitement gradually cooled...
...I heard, with perfect clarity, the voice of my own heart.
I want to be with Satoshi-kun.
And so?
I want him to stay on the baseball team.
And so?
I want to remove the source of anguish that's tormenting him.
Which means?
...Which means... what do I do?
As the buzzing in my head faded away... the distant voice of the umpire reached my ears.
``Game set!@k@vS06/00/judge15002. That's the game!!``
